rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks for component ports must be placed in component type scopes, not implementation scopes
Implementation-level AGREE bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections are defined
generic_rules: Component type contracts should reference port variables through valid component type declarations
AGREE annex blocks must preserve the architectural scope and ownership of the target component or implementation
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use valid AADL scoping rules when placing AGREE annex blocks in component types or implementations
Ensure helper declarations match the expected types (bool, int, real) for comparison expressions
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level AGREE bindings in component type scopes
Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts without proper variable representation
irrelevant_topics: model repair patterns and validator-driven fixes
